Pregnancy Test 
To use the sharing features on this page, please enable JavaScript. 
What is a pregnancy test? 
A pregnancy Â test can tell whether you're pregnant by checking a sample of your urine (pee) or blood for a hormone called human chorionic gonadotropin (hCG).
This hormone Â is made by the placenta, the organ that grows in your uterus to provide oxygen and nutrients to the fetus. The placenta starts making hCG after a fertilized egg attaches to the inside wall of you
